title: iberis dunalii
intro: Iberis dunalii, also known as Candytuft, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, Iberis dunalii has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in digestion. Iberis dunalii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
